conviction.low: You hold your beliefs loosely. This keeps you intellectually mobile and avoids rigidity — but loosely-held everything can make it hard to commit to a direction under pressure.
conviction.mid: You hold beliefs with calibration: firm where the evidence is strong, loose where it is not. This is the productive middle of conviction.
conviction.high: You hold your beliefs firmly. This sharpens commitment and coherence under pressure. Strength without a revision path becomes rigidity — pair it with openness.

revisionOpenness.low: You are resistant to revising beliefs once held. Whatever its source — confidence, identity, or fatigue — this protects coherence but raises the cost of being wrong.
revisionOpenness.mid: You are selectively open to revision — willing where the stakes are empirical, protective where the belief is a value.
revisionOpenness.high: You are broadly open to revising beliefs. This is intellectually healthy; the warning is treating openness as a virtue in itself, where revision becomes performative rather than earned.

evidenceSensitivity.low: Your revision conditions are not tightly tied to empirical evidence — you may revise for social pressure, authority, or not at all. Watch what you actually let count as a reason.
evidenceSensitivity.mid: You let evidence matter, but not infinitely: a credible counterexample can move you, while noise cannot.
evidenceSensitivity.high: Your beliefs are tightly pegged to evidence: you revise on credible counterexamples and independent studies. Healthy — provided your bar for “credible” is itself calibrated and not a gatekeeping move.

valueAnchoring.low: Few of your beliefs are anchored as core values — most are open to evidence. This keeps you adaptable; it can also mean you lack the stable commitments a long mission needs.
valueAnchoring.mid: You distinguish principles from applications: you will revise how a belief applies but protect the principle itself. This is often the most durable stance.
valueAnchoring.high: Several of your beliefs are anchored as values you will not revise on evidence. This is legitimate for genuine values — but verify they are values and not beliefs with value’s costume.

tension.high_conviction_high_openness: You hold beliefs firmly and revise them readily. This is the ideal of strong conviction paired with calibrated openness — rare, and worth watching for unevenness across topics.
tension.high_conviction_low_openness: You hold beliefs firmly and resist revision. This is coherence under pressure — but it raises the cost of being wrong. Name a belief or two where you would welcome a counterexample.
tension.low_conviction_high_openness: You hold beliefs loosely and revise readily. Intellectually mobile — but check that openness is not masking a lack of committed direction.
tension.default: Your tension between conviction and openness differs across topics. The useful question is not the average but the shape: where are you firm-and-open (healthy), and where firm-and-closed (risk)?

shareTexts.invite: What would actually change your mind?
shareTexts.minimal: I mapped my Evidence Revision Profile. What would change yours?

recommendation.label: Outfinity initiative
recommendation.title: Explorer Circle
recommendation.text: Explorer Circle is a community for builders practicing honest revision — holding direction while remaining open to the evidence that should change it. You do not need to share your result to participate.
recommendation.note: Opening Explorer Circle takes you to Substack. Your result is not transmitted.
